title: ceratostema costatum
intro: Ceratostema costatum, also known as the Sipapai or 'Māori Raspberry', is a flowering plant native to New Zealand and parts of the Pacific Islands. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ceratostema costatum include its ability to help lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and poultices, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
